title: IacApplyRecord
description: Audit record written by bin/iac-apply.sh AFTER all gates pass and the tool apply has been invoked. Lives at memory/.nyann/iac-applies/<ISO-ts>/manifest.json (analogous to a BootRecord). Records WHAT was applied — tool, unit, the plan's add/change/destroy summary, the gate flags that were satisfied, and the apply command's exit status — so a teammate can audit the change after the fact. CRITICAL PRIVACY INVARIANT: this record contains NO credentials, NO secrets, NO full state, and NO raw plan/state file contents. nyann shells out to the user's already-authenticated CLI and never reads/stores/logs/passes credentials. The record stores only metadata (counts, basenames, exit code, the plan's sha256), never the .tfstate / .tfvars / plan-binary bytes. additionalProperties:false enforces the closed shape so a leak can't sneak in via an extra key.

schema_version.description: IacApplyRecord schema version. Bump in lockstep with the schema's $id when v2 ships.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when iac-apply.sh began the apply.

target.description: Repo basename at record time (NOT an absolute path). Mirrors BootRecord.target — the absolute path would leak the operator's username and filesystem layout to anyone the record is shared with.

tool.description: The iac.tool that was applied (long-form, schema-aligned).

unit.description: Repo-relative path of the applied unit ('.' for root, or the whole target when no --unit was scoped).

summary.description: The add/change/destroy counts from the IacPlan that gated this apply. For advisory tools these are 0 (the structured count was unavailable).

destructive.description: Whether the gating plan was destructive (summary.destroy > 0, or advisory destructive_known:false treated as potentially-destructive).

destructive_known.description: Whether the destructive verdict came from a structured plan (true) or an advisory text diff (false). Recorded for audit so a reviewer knows the confidence of the destroy count.

plan_sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the canonicalised IacPlan JSON that gated this apply. Binds the audit record to the exact preview the operator confirmed. NOT a hash of any state/secret — only of the normalized, credential-free IacPlan.

gates.description: Which safety gates were required and satisfied for this apply. An apply record only exists when every required gate was satisfied — this block records the proof.

apply_flag.description: True — --apply was explicitly passed. Always true in a written record (no record is written for a preview).

confirm_destroy.description: True when --confirm-destroy was required (destructive plan) AND passed. False when the plan was non-destructive (the flag was not required).

confirmation_guard.description: Result of bin/guards/iac-apply-confirmation.sh — 'pass' (gate satisfied), or 'not-required' for a non-destructive plan where the guard is a no-op.

apply.description: Outcome of the tool apply invocation. Stores the exit code and a redacted, one-line command shape — NEVER the full argv with secret-bearing var values, NEVER the tool's stdout/stderr (which can echo state).

command.description: The tool subcommand shape that was run (e.g. 'terraform apply <plan>', 'pulumi up'). A descriptive label, not the literal argv — no var-file values, no -var assignments, no env.

exit_code.description: Exit code of the apply command. 0 = success; non-zero = the tool reported failure (recorded honestly for audit).

message.description: Human-readable one-line outcome summary. Must not echo tool output.
